routes.documentation: Endpoint performing CRUD operations on a data bucket (automatically creates GET, POST, PUT, DELETE routes)

responses.label: Perform CRUD operations on the "Users" databucket ("Data" tab at the top)

routes.documentation: Generate random body (JSON, text, CSV, etc) with templating

responses.label: Creates 10 random users, or the amount specified in the 'total' query param
Default response
Content XYZ
Content not found

responses.body:
The current path will match the following routes: 
http://localhost:3000/path/with/pattern/
http://localhost:3000/path/with/patterns/
http://localhost:3000/path/with/patterns/anything-else

Learn more about Mockoon's routing: https://mockoon.com/docs/latest/api-endpoints/routing/

routes.documentation: "Guard" route protecting all routes starting with /protected/

responses.label: Requires the presence of an 'Authorization' header

responses.body:
You can serve the same responses based on the same rules for all or part of your endpoints by creating global routes using the fallback mode and a wildcard path. 
This is useful if you want to protect all your endpoints by checking if an Authorization header is present or if you want to verify that all your requests contain a specific property in their body.
To learn more: https://mockoon.com/docs/latest/route-responses/global-routes-with-rules/

routes.documentation: Can Mockoon forward or record entering requests?

responses.body:
Mockoon can also act as a proxy and forward all entering requests that are not caught by declared routes. 
You can activate this option in the environment settings ("Settings" tab at the top). 
To learn more: https://mockoon.com/docs/latest/server-configuration/proxy-mode/

All entering requests, and responses from the proxied server will be recorded and can be automatically mocked ("Logs" tab at the top).
To learn more: https://mockoon.com/docs/latest/logging-and-recording/requests-logging/
